command:
ssh -i /home/jose/.ssh/n8n_bridge -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 jose@192.168.0.199 bash << 'REMOTE'
echo "=== openclaw-gateway service ==="
systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway 2>/dev/null || systemctl is-active openclaw-gateway 2>/dev/null || echo "checking user service..."
systemctl --user status openclaw-gateway 2>/dev/null | grep -E "Active:|Main PID:" | head -2

echo "=== port 9119 listening? ==="
ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep 9119 || sudo ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep 9119 || echo "9119 NOT listening"

echo "=== Nginx 443 ==="
sudo ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':443' | head -1 || echo "443 not listening"

echo "=== Gateway health (local) ==="
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "local 9119: %{http_code}\n" --max-time 8 https://localhost:9119/ 2>/dev/null
curl -sk -o /dev/null -w "local 443: %{http_code}\n" --max-time 8 https://localhost:443/ 2>/dev/null

echo "=== Telegram bot @RemyDeviDollz_bot alive? ==="
# find remy bot token if present
REMY_TOKEN=$(grep -rhoE "[0-9]{8,10}:[A-Za-z0-9_-]{35}" /home/jose/.openclaw/ 2>/dev/null | head -1)
if [ -n "$REMY_TOKEN" ]; then
  curl -s "https://api.telegram.org/bot${REMY_TOKEN}/getMe" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); r=d.get('result',{}); print('bot:', r.get('username'), 'ok:', d.get('ok'))" 2>/dev/null
else
  echo "no remy telegram token found in config"
fi
REMOTE
